children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Within the program authorized under , the Secretary is authorized to establish a pilot program, to be known as the “Recompete Pilot Program”, to provide grants to eligible recipients representing eligible areas or Tribal lands to alleviate persistent economic distress and support long-term comprehensive economic development and job creation in eligible areas.section 3722a of this title

children.heading: Strategy development grants and cooperative agreements
children.label: b Strategy development grants and cooperative agreements
children.text: Subject to available appropriations, the Secretary is authorized, on the application of an eligible recipient, to award up to one half of the number of grants under subsection (e) of to eligible recipients to develop a recompete plan and carry out related predevelopment activities.section 3722a of this title

children.heading: Strategy implementation grants and cooperative agreements
children.label: c Strategy implementation grants and cooperative agreements
A
children.text: customized job training programs carried out by local community colleges and other training or educational organizations in partnership with local businesses;

children.label: B
children.text: workforce outreach programs located in, and targeted to, lower-income and underemployed neighborhoods; and

children.label: C
children.text: programs to embed job placement and training services in neighborhood institutions such as churches, housing projects, and community advocacy programs; and

children.label: D
children.text: job retention programs and activities, such as the provision of career coaches;

children.label: D

children.label: E

children.label: F
children.text: assistance in accessing capital;

infrastructure related activities of the kind described in or other land and site development programs, such as brownfield redevelopment, research and technology parks, business incubators, business corridor development, and other infrastructure activities related to supporting job creation and employment for residents, subject to the requirements of ; andsection 3722a(f) of this titlesection 3722a(f)(6) of this title

additional planning, predevelopment, technical assistance, and other administrative activities as may be necessary for the ongoing implementation, administration, and operation of the programs and activities carried out with a grant or cooperative agreement under this section, including but not limited to economic development planning and evaluation.

children.heading: Initial performance period
children.label: 1 Initial performance period
children.text: The term of an initial grant or cooperative agreement awarded under subsection (c) shall be for a period that the Secretary deems appropriate for the proposed activities but not less than 2 years.

children.heading: Subsequent performance period
children.label: 2 Subsequent performance period
children.text: The Secretary may renew a grant or cooperative agreement awarded under subsection (c) for such period, such amount, and such terms as the Secretary considers appropriate, if the Secretary determines that the recipient of an award under subsection (c) has made satisfactory progress towards metrics or benchmarking requirements established by the Secretary at time of award.

children.heading: Flexible approach
children.label: 3 Flexible approach
children.text: In renewing a grant or cooperative agreement under subsection (c), the Secretary may approve new or additional uses of funds, consistent with the uses described in subsection (c), to meet changes in the needs of the region.

children.heading: Limitation on eligible areas
children.label: 1 Limitation on eligible areas
children.text: An eligible area may not benefit from more than 1 grant or cooperative agreement described in subsection (b) and 1 grant or cooperative agreement described in subsection (c), provided that a renewal described in subsection (d)(2) shall not constitute an additional grant.

children.heading: Limitation on recipients
children.label: 2 Limitation on recipients
children.text: For purposes of the program under this section, an eligible recipient may not receive multiple grants described in subsection (c) on behalf of more than 1 eligible area.

children.label: A
children.text: take into consideration the proposed activities and projected expenditures outlined in an approved recompete plan; and

children.label: i
children.text: the prime-age employment gap of the eligible area;

children.label: ii
children.text: the prime-age population of the eligible area; and

children.label: I
children.text: $70,585 for local labor markets; or

children.label: II
children.text: $53,600 for local communities.

children.heading: Minimum amount
children.label: 2 Minimum amount
children.text: The Secretary may not make an award that is less than $20,000,000 to an eligible recipient.

subsection (b) of this section, an eligible recipient shall submit to the Secretary an application at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary determines to be appropriate; and

subsection (c) of this section, an eligible recipient shall submit to the Secretary an application at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary determines to be appropriate, including a recompete plan approved by the Secretary.

children.heading: Relation to certain grant awards
children.label: h Relation to certain grant awards
children.text: The Secretary shall not require an eligible recipient to receive a grant or cooperative agreement under subsection (b) in order to receive a grant or cooperative agreement under subsection (c).

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: i Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary $1,000,000,000 to award grants and cooperative agreements under subsection (c) of this section, for the period of fiscal years 2022 through 2026.

children.label: i
children.text: has a prime-age employment gap equal to not less than 2.5 percent; and

children.label: ii
children.text: meets additional criteria as the Secretary may establish.

children.label: i
children.text: has a prime-age employment gap equal to not less than 5 percent;

children.label: ii
children.text: is not located within an eligible local labor market that meets the criteria described in subparagraph (A);

children.label: iii
children.text: has a median annual household income of not more than $75,000; and

children.label: iv
children.text: meets additional criteria as the Secretary may establish.

children.heading: Eligible recipient
children.label: 2 Eligible recipient
children.text: The term “eligible recipient” means a specified entity that has been authorized in a manner as determined by the Secretary to represent and act on behalf of an eligible area for the purposes of this section.

children.label: A
children.text: A metropolitan statistical area or micropolitan statistical area, excluding any area described in subparagraph (C).

children.label: B
children.text: A commuting zone, excluding any areas described in subparagraphs (A) and (C).

children.label: C
children.text: The Tribal land with a Tribal prime-age population represented by a Tribal government.

children.heading: Local community
children.label: 4 Local community
children.text: The term “local community” means the area served by a general-purpose unit of local government that is located within, but does not cover the entire area of, a local labor market that does not meet the criteria described in paragraph (1)(A).

children.label: i
children.text: the national 5-year average prime-age employment rate; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the 5-year average prime-age employment rate of the eligible area.

children.heading: Calculation
children.label: B Calculation
children.text: For the purposes of subparagraph (A), an individual is prime-age if such individual between the ages of 25 years and 54 years.

children.label: i
children.text: proposed programs and activities to be carried out with a grant awarded under subsection (c) to address the economic challenges of the eligible area in a comprehensive manner that promotes long-term, sustained economic growth, lasting job creation, per capita wage increases, and reduction in the prime-age employment gap of the eligible area;

children.label: ii
children.text: projected costs and annual expenditures and proposed disbursement schedule;

children.label: iii
children.text: the roles and responsibilities of specified entities that may receive grant funds awarded under subsection (c); and

children.label: iv
children.text: other information as the Secretary determines appropriate;

children.label: B

children.label: C
children.text: may be modified over the term of the grant by the eligible recipient, subject to the approval of the Secretary or at the direction of the Secretary, if the Secretary determines benchmarking requirements are repeatedly not met or if other circumstances necessitate a modification.

children.heading: Use of data
children.label: B Use of data
children.text: A calculation under subparagraph (A) shall be determined based on data provided by the applicable Tribal government to the Department of the Treasury under the Coronavirus State and Local Fiscal Recovery Fund programs under title VI of the Social Security Act ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 801
